title: clinopodium capitatum
intro: Clinopodium capitatum, also known as New Zealand knotweed or mountain mint, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand and Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of essential oils such as camphor and thymol. The health benefits of Clinopodium capitatum include relief from respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. Herbal preparations of Clinopodium capitatum include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
